   <xs:simpleType name="playbackRealtimeMultipleType">     <xs:simpleType name="playbackRealtimeMultipleType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>playbackRealtimeMultiple is a datatype for setting the simulation time to realtime multiplier for the rate of playing back recorded messages in a C2SIM coalition of systems. It is defined as a positive decimal type. For example, a value of 2.5 indicates playback of recorded messages at 2.5 times faster than realtime. A value of 0.5 indicates playback of recorded messages at half the realtime clock, or running half the speed of realtime.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>playbackRealtimeMultiple is a datatype for setting the simulation time to realtime multiplier for the rate of playing back recorded messages in a C2SIM coalition of systems. It is defined as a positive decimal type. For example, a value of 2.5 indicates playback of recorded messages at 2.5 times faster than realtime. A value of 0.5 indicates playback of recorded messages at half the realtime clock, or running half the speed of realtime.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#playbackRealtimeMultiple</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#playbackRealtimeMultiple</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="xs:decimal">        <xs:restriction base="xs:decimal"> 
         <xs:minExclusive value="0.0"/>           <xs:minExclusive value="0.0"/> 
      </xs:restriction>        </xs:restriction> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:simpleType name="simulationRealtimeMultipleType">     <xs:simpleType name="simulationRealtimeMultipleType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>simulationRealtimeMultiple is a datatype for setting the simulation time to realtime multiplier. It is defined as a positive decimal type. For example, a value of 2.5 indicates a simulation is advancing the simulation time 2.5 times faster than realtime. A value of 0.5 indicates a simulation is advancing the simulation time at half the realtime clock, or running half the speed of realtime. For example, a slower rate is sometimes used to slow down a simulation to allow more time for human operator interaction.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>simulationRealtimeMultiple is a datatype for setting the simulation time to realtime multiplier. It is defined as a positive decimal type. For example, a value of 2.5 indicates a simulation is advancing the simulation time 2.5 times faster than realtime. A value of 0.5 indicates a simulation is advancing the simulation time at half the realtime clock, or running half the speed of realtime. For example, a slower rate is sometimes used to slow down a simulation to allow more time for human operator interaction.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#simulationRealtimeMultiple</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#simulationRealtimeMultiple</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="xs:decimal">        <xs:restriction base="xs:decimal"> 
         <xs:minExclusive value="0.0"/>           <xs:minExclusive value="0.0"/> 
      </xs:restriction>        </xs:restriction> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType>

   <xs:simpleType name="PlaybackRealtimeMultipleType">     <xs:simpleType name="PlaybackRealtimeMultipleType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Decimal rate indicating ratio of simulation time to real time for playback of recorded messages (e.g., value of 2.0 indicates messages are played back at 2:1 simulation time to realtime [i.e., faster than realtime]; 0.5 indicates message playback at half realtime [i.e., slower than realtime]).</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Decimal rate indicating ratio of simulation time to real time for playback of recorded messages (e.g., value of 2.0 indicates messages are played back at 2:1 simulation time to realtime [i.e., faster than realtime]; 0.5 indicates message playback at half realtime [i.e., slower than realtime]).</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#hasPlaybackRealtimeMultiple</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#hasPlaybackRealtimeMultiple</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="playbackRealtimeMultipleType"/>        <xs:restriction base="playbackRealtimeMultipleType"/> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:element name="PlaybackRealtimeMultiple" type="PlaybackRealtimeMultipleType"/>     <xs:element name="PlaybackRealtimeMultiple" type="PlaybackRealtimeMultipleType"/>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Decimal rate indicating ratio of simulation time to real time (e.g., value of 2.0 indicates simulation is executing at 2:1 simulation time to realtime [i.e., faster than realtime]; 0.5 indicates simulation  is executing at half realtime [i.e., slower than realtime]).</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Decimal rate indicating ratio of simulation time to real time (e.g., value of 2.0 indicates simulation is executing at 2:1 simulation time to realtime [i.e., faster than realtime]; 0.5 indicates simulation  is executing at half realtime [i.e., slower than realtime]).</xs:documentation>

Used in reporting the status of a current playback of recorded C2SIM messages.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Gives the time duration (isoTimeDurationBase) since the start time of the current playback; i.e., the message timestamp that the playback has reached. Used in reporting the status of a current playback of recorded C2SIM messages.</xs:documentation>

.         <xs:documentation>The reference is to an instance of Entity that sent associated information.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>UUIDBase. Reference to an instance of Entity.  
  This property should reference the operational entity that sends the message.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isFromSender</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isFromSender</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType>

.         <xs:documentation>Unique FIPA identifer of the system from which individual message is sent.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>String. Name of the system from which individual message is sent.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isFromSendingSystem</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isFromSendingSystem</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="xs:string"/>        <xs:restriction base="xs:string"/> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType>

   <xs:simpleType name="InReplyToMessageIDType">     <xs:simpleType name="InReplyToMessageIDType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
.         <xs:documentation>Unique FIPA identifer of the message to be identified in a message reply.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>UUID. Unique identifier of the message to be identified in a message reply.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isInReplyToMessageID</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isInReplyToMessageID</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="UUIDBaseType"/>        <xs:restriction base="UUIDBaseType"/> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:element name="InReplyToMessageID" type="InReplyToMessageIDType"/>     <xs:element name="InReplyToMessageID" type="InReplyToMessageIDType"/> 
   <xs:simpleType name="ToReceiverType">     <xs:simpleType name="ToReceiverType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
.         <xs:documentation>The reference is to an instance of Entity that intended to receive the associated information.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>UUIDBase. List of references to instances of Entity.  
  If this property is set, then the sender considers that this message should only be distributed to these referenced operational entities.
  If this property is not set, then the sender considers that the message should be broadcast to all units on the same side as the sender.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isToReceiver</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isToReceiver</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="UUIDBaseType"/>        <xs:restriction base="UUIDBaseType"/> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:element name="ToReceiver" type="ToReceiverType"/>     <xs:element name="ToReceiver" type="ToReceiverType"/> 
   <xs:simpleType name="ToReceivingSystemType">     <xs:simpleType name="ToReceivingSystemType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
.         <xs:documentation>Unique FIPA identifer of the system to which individual message is sent.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>List of strings. Name(s) of the system(s) to which the individual message is addressed, for example in a reply message by using the ReplyToSystem property value of the request message.  
  If one or more system is set in the message header, then the message MUST be distributed ONLY to these systems. If no system is set in the message header, the message MUST be distributed to ALL systems potentially interested by it.  
  The definition of â€œinterestâ€? may vary depending on the capabilities actually available in the system distributing the messages (server in a client/server architecture, message broker, etc.), and is therefore left to the discretion of the system distributing the messages. In some cases, messages could be distributed to all C2SIM participants.  
  For operational messages (with a DomainMessageBody), the concept of interest (implemented by a server or a message-broker) MUST only be defined with operational data, such as the type of message, the sender (operational unit), the receiver (operational unit) or the network (operational communication network).</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isToReceivingSystem</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#isToReceivingSystem</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="xs:string"/>        <xs:restriction base="xs:string"/> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:element name="ToReceivingSystem" type="ToReceivingSystemType"/>     <xs:element name="ToReceivingSystem" type="ToReceivingSystemType"/> 
   <xs:simpleType name="FunctionalAssociationWithTaskType">     <xs:simpleType name="FunctionalAssociationWithTaskType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This property identifies the UUID of a Maneuver Warfare Task that is the object of a JC3IEDM ActionFunctionalAssociation.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This property identifies the UUID of a Maneuver Warfare Task that is the object of a JC3IEDM ActionFunctionalAssociation.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#hasFunctionalAssociationWithTask</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#hasFunctionalAssociationWithTask</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType>

   <xs:element name="EndTime" type="TimeInstantType">     <xs:element name="EndTime" type="TimeInstantType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The ending  Time Instant of any concept that exists over an interval of time. For example, the period of effectiveness of an Action/Task (C-BML)</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The ending  Time Instant of any concept that exists over an interval of time.    <xs:group name="AbstractObjectGroup">     <xs:group name="AbstractObjectGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An AbstractObject is an object in the system that does not have the full characteristics of an entity. For example, it does not have a location or an extent. It does however have a unique identifier.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An AbstractObject is an object in the system that does not have the full characteristics of an entity. For example, it does not have a location or an extent. It does however have a unique identifier.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AbstractObject</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AbstractObject</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="UUID" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="UUID" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group> 
   <xs:complexType name="AbstractObjectType">     <xs:complexType name="AbstractObjectType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An AbstractObject is an object in the system that does not have the full characteristics of an entity. For example, it does not have a location or an extent. It does however have a unique identifier.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An AbstractObject is an object in the system that does not have the full characteristics of an entity. For example, it does not have a location or an extent. It does however have a unique identifier.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AbstractObject</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AbstractObject</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="AbstractOrganization"/>           <xs:element ref="AbstractOrganization"/> 
         <xs:element ref="CommunicationNetwork"/>           <xs:element ref="CommunicationNetwork"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ForceSide"/>           <xs:element ref="ForceSide"/>

   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="AbstractOrganizationType">     <xs:complexType name="AbstractOrganizationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A group that an actor can be affiliated with. Does not perform any actions and does not have a specific location.          <xs:documentation>A group that an actor can be affiliated with. Does not perform any actions and does not have a specific location.
Subclasses of this class can be created for country, religion, etc. Each of these will have defined instances (one for each country, etc.)</xs:documentation>  Subclasses of this class can be created for country, religion, etc. Each of these will have defined instances (one for each country, etc.)</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AbstractOrganization</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AbstractOrganization</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="AbstractObjectGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="AbstractObjectGroup"/> 
         <xs:element ref="CountryCode" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="CountryCode" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="EthnicGroupCode" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="EthnicGroupCode" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="OrganizationTypeCode" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="OrganizationTypeCode" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ReligionCode" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="ReligionCode" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="AbstractOrganization" type="AbstractOrganizationType"/>     <xs:element name="AbstractOrganization" type="AbstractOrganizationType"/>

         <xs:enumeration value="ACKRCVD"/>           <xs:enumeration value="ACKRCVD"/>

         <xs:enumeration value="ACKSUCC"/>           <xs:enumeration value="ACKSUCC"/>

         <xs:documentation>An ActorEntity is an entity that can send/receive orders and reports and/or perform tasks.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An ActorEntity is an entity that can send/receive orders and reports and/or perform tasks.</xs:documentation>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This is a relationship of friendly, hostile, or neutral toward an actor entity. It is part of the definition of the hasAllegianceTo property of an EntityDescriptor.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This is a relationship of friendly, hostile, or neutral toward an actor entity. It is part of the definition of the hasAllegianceTo property of an EntityDescriptor.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AllegianceRelationship</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AllegianceRelationship</xs:documentation>

         <xs:documentation>This code defines the types of allegiance in an AllegianceRelationship.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This code defines the types of allegiance in an AllegianceRelationship.</xs:documentation>

         <xs:enumeration value="HostileTo"/>           <xs:enumeration value="HostileTo"/> 
         <xs:enumeration value="NeutralTo"/>           <xs:enumeration value="NeutralTo"/>

      </xs:restriction>        </xs:restriction> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:complexType name="AuthorizationHeaderType">     <xs:complexType name="AuthorizationHeaderType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The Authorization header is a part of the C2SIM headers used in client-server communications. Its primary function is to authenticate a user-agent with a server, typically by carrying credentials in the form of a token or a set of credentials like username and password. This header is fundamental in implementing security measures for web applications and APIs.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The Authorization header is a part of the C2SIM headers used in client-server communications. Its primary function is to authenticate a user-agent with a server, typically by carrying credentials in the form of a token or a set of credentials like username and password. This header is fundamental in implementing security measures for web applications and APIs.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AuthorizationHeader</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#AuthorizationHeader</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="AuthorizationCredentials" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="AuthorizationCredentials" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="AuthorizationType" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="AuthorizationType" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>

   <xs:complexType name="CheckpointRestoreType">     <xs:complexType name="CheckpointRestoreType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The CheckpointRestore system command directs systems in a C2SIM coalition to restore their state to the saved data in the named checkpoint file.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The CheckpointRestore system command directs systems in a C2SIM coalition to restore their state to the saved data in the named checkpoint file.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CheckpointRestore</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CheckpointRestore</xs:documentation>

   <xs:complexType name="CheckpointSaveType">     <xs:complexType name="CheckpointSaveType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The CheckpointSave system command directs systems in a C2SIM coalition to save their current state. The name is used to distinguish the current set of saved data from other named checkpoints.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The CheckpointSave system command directs systems in a C2SIM coalition to save their current state. The name is used to distinguish the current set of saved data from other named checkpoints.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CheckpointSave</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CheckpointSave</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

         <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A collection of enumerations used by various data types in C2SIM</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A collection of enumerations used by various data types in C2SIM</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Code</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Code</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="ActionCode"/>           <xs:element ref="ActionCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ActionTemporalAssociationCode"/>           <xs:element ref="ActionTemporalAssociationCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="AllegianceRelationshipCode"/>           <xs:element ref="AllegianceRelationshipCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="CommandRelationCode"/>           <xs:element ref="CommandRelationCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="DesiredEffectCode"/>           <xs:element ref="DesiredEffectCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="EchelonCode"/>           <xs:element ref="EchelonCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="HostilityStatusCode"/>           <xs:element ref="HostilityStatusCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="OperationalStatusCode"/>           <xs:element ref="OperationalStatusCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="OrganizationCode"/>           <xs:element ref="OrganizationCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseCompletionCondition"/>           <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseCompletionCondition"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ReinforcedReducedType"/>           <xs:element ref="ReinforcedReducedType"/> 
.         <xs:element ref="SecurityClassificationCode"/>   
         <xs:element ref="TaskFunctionalAssociationCode"/>           <xs:element ref="TaskFunctionalAssociationCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="TimeReferenceCode"/>           <xs:element ref="TimeReferenceCode"/> 
         <xs:element ref="WeaponRuleOfEngagementCode"/>           <xs:element ref="WeaponRuleOfEngagementCode"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:group name="CollectiveEntityGroup">     <xs:group name="CollectiveEntityGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This is an Entity that is normally thought of as having constituent entities, i.e. a Unit.          <xs:documentation>This is an Entity that is normally thought of as having constituent entities, i.e. a Unit.
   
CollectiveEntities may or may not hve their subordinates explicitly represented in the exercise (i.e., it may always be aggregated, or it may have subordinates that are themselves entities).  If they are represented, then the hasSubordinate property will have at least one instance. CollectiveEntities may or may not hve their subordinates explicitly represented in the exercise (i.e., it may always be aggregated, or it may have subordinates that are themselves entities).  If they are represented, then the hasSubordinate property will have at least one instance.
   
The CollectivEntity may be an abstract actor, or it may have physical properties; if the latter, then the hasCurrentState property is present with a PhysicalState.</xs:documentation>  The CollectivEntity may be an abstract actor, or it may have physical properties; if the latter, then the hasCurrentState property is present with a PhysicalState.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CollectiveEntity</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CollectiveEntity</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="CurrentState" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="CurrentState" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Subordinate" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="Subordinate" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group> 
   <xs:complexType name="CollectiveEntityType">     <xs:complexType name="CollectiveEntityType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This is an Entity that is normally thought of as having constituent entities, i.e. a Unit.          <xs:documentation>This is an Entity that is normally thought of as having constituent entities, i.e. a Unit.
   
CollectiveEntities may or may not hve their subordinates explicitly represented in the exercise (i.e., it may always be aggregated, or it may have subordinates that are themselves entities).  If they are represented, then the hasSubordinate property will have at least one instance. CollectiveEntities may or may not hve their subordinates explicitly represented in the exercise (i.e., it may always be aggregated, or it may have subordinates that are themselves entities).  If they are represented, then the hasSubordinate property will have at least one instance.
   
The CollectivEntity may be an abstract actor, or it may have physical properties; if the latter, then the hasCurrentState property is present with a PhysicalState.</xs:documentation>  The CollectivEntity may be an abstract actor, or it may have physical properties; if the latter, then the hasCurrentState property is present with a PhysicalState.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CollectiveEntity</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CollectiveEntity</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="MilitaryOrganization"/>           <xs:element ref="MilitaryOrganization"/> 
         <xs:element ref="NonMilitaryOrganization"/>           <xs:element ref="NonMilitaryOrganization"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="CollectiveEntity" type="CollectiveEntityType"/>     <xs:element name="CollectiveEntity" type="CollectiveEntityType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="CommunicationNetworkType">     <xs:complexType name="CommunicationNetworkType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An specific network over which an entity communicates. A logical entity. Will have data properties defining communication parameters.          <xs:documentation>An specific network over which an entity communicates. A logical entity. Will have data properties defining communication parameters.
Networks are part of an EntityDescriptor. If a message is sent to a CommunicationsNetwork, all ActorEntities that have this network will receive the message.</xs:documentation>  Networks are part of an EntityDescriptor. If a message is sent to a CommunicationsNetwork, all ActorEntities that have this network will receive the message.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CommunicationNetwork</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CommunicationNetwork</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="AbstractObjectGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="AbstractObjectGroup"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="CommunicationNetwork" type="CommunicationNetworkType"/>     <xs:element name="CommunicationNetwork" type="CommunicationNetworkType"/> 
.   <xs:simpleType name="CommunicativeActTypeCodeType">   
      <xs:annotation>   
         <xs:documentation>Enumeration of different types of communications.</xs:documentation>   
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CommunicativeActTypeCode</xs:documentation>   
      </xs:annotation>   
      <xs:restriction base="xs:string">   
         <xs:enumeration value="Accept"/>   
         <xs:enumeration value="Agree"/>   
         <xs:enumeration value="Confirm"/>   
         <xs:enumeration value="Inform"/>   
         <xs:enumeration value="Propose"/>   
         <xs:enumeration value="Refuse"/>   
         <xs:enumeration value="Request"/>   
      </xs:restriction>   
   </xs:simpleType>   
   <xs:simpleType name="CountryCodeType">     <xs:simpleType name="CountryCodeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A unique three-letter code for each nation on Earth, defined in ISO 3166-1 standard and also found in JC3IEDM AffiliationGeopoliticalCode.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A unique three-letter code for each nation on Earth, defined in ISO 3166-1 standard and also found in JC3IEDM AffiliationGeopoliticalCode.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CountryCode</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#CountryCode</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

   <xs:complexType name="DateTimeType">     <xs:complexType name="DateTimeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A date and time in ISO 8601:2000 DateTime Group format yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ssZ e.g. 1998-05-12T14:15:00Z. From MSDL simple types.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A date and time in ISO 8601:2000 DateTime Group format yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ssZ e.g. 1998-05-12T14:15:00Z. From MSDL simple types.</xs:documentation>

   <xs:group name="DomainMessageBodyGroup">     <xs:group name="DomainMessageBodyGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The body of a message that is sent between actors in the domain.          <xs:documentation>The body of a message that is sent between actors in the domain.
It is one subclass of MessageBody (other subclasses perform administrative functions in the C2SIM Coalition.)</xs:documentation>  It is one subclass of MessageBody (other subclasses perform administrative functions in the C2SIM Coalition.)</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#DomainMessageBody</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#DomainMessageBody</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="FromSender" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="FromSender" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
.         <xs:element ref="ToReceiver" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="ToReceiver" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group> 
   <xs:complexType name="DomainMessageBodyType">     <xs:complexType name="DomainMessageBodyType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The body of a message that is sent between actors in the domain.          <xs:documentation>The body of a message that is sent between actors in the domain.
It is one subclass of MessageBody (other subclasses perform administrative functions in the C2SIM Coalition.)</xs:documentation>  It is one subclass of MessageBody (other subclasses perform administrative functions in the C2SIM Coalition.)</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#DomainMessageBody</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#DomainMessageBody</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="AcknowledgementBody"/>           <xs:element ref="AcknowledgementBody"/> 
         <xs:element ref="OrderBody"/>           <xs:element ref="OrderBody"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PlanBody"/>           <xs:element ref="PlanBody"/>

         <xs:element ref="RequestBody"/>           <xs:element ref="RequestBody"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="DomainMessageBody" type="DomainMessageBodyType"/>     <xs:element name="DomainMessageBody" type="DomainMessageBodyType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="DurationType">     <xs:complexType name="DurationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A time duration expressed in ISO 8601:2000 duration format PnYnMnDTnHnMnS e.g. P00Y00M00DT00H00M00S. Note that this does not support fractions of seconds. From MSDL simple types.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A time duration expressed in ISO 8601:2000 duration format PnYnMnDTnHnMnS e.g. P00Y00M00DT00H00M00S. Note that this does not support fractions of seconds. From MSDL simple types.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Duration</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Duration</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="IsoTimeDuration" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="IsoTimeDuration" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:group name="EntityGroup">     <xs:group name="EntityGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An individually identified object. Objects may have physical properties, in which case they belong to the subclass ConcreteEntity. Objects may be able to perform actions, in which case they belong in the subclass ActorEntity. If they have physical properties AND can perform actions, then they inherit from both subclasses.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An individually identified object. Objects may have physical properties, in which case they belong to the subclass ConcreteEntity. Objects may be able to perform actions, in which case they belong in the subclass ActorEntity. If they have physical properties AND can perform actions, then they inherit from both subclasses.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Entity</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Entity</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="APP6CSymbol" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="APP6CSymbol" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="NamedEntityType" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="NamedEntityType" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="SISOEntityType" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="SISOEntityType" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="UUID" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="UUID" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group> 
   <xs:complexType name="EntityType">     <xs:complexType name="EntityType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An individually identified object. Objects may have physical properties, in which case they belong to the subclass ConcreteEntity. Objects may be able to perform actions, in which case they belong in the subclass ActorEntity. If they have physical properties AND can perform actions, then they inherit from both subclasses.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An individually identified object. Objects may have physical properties, in which case they belong to the subclass ConcreteEntity. Objects may be able to perform actions, in which case they belong in the subclass ActorEntity. If they have physical properties AND can perform actions, then they inherit from both subclasses.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Entity</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Entity</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="ActorEntity"/>           <xs:element ref="ActorEntity"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PhysicalEntity"/>           <xs:element ref="PhysicalEntity"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="EntityDescriptorType">     <xs:complexType name="EntityDescriptorType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The Descriptor defines static attributes of an entity, i.e. that do not change during the course of the simulation. This information is provided at initialization time.          <xs:documentation>The Descriptor defines static attributes of an entity, i.e. that do not change during the course of the simulation. This information is provided at initialization time.
   
The friend/hostile/... relationship of this entity with others can be specfied with hasAllegianceRelationship properties and/or the hasSide property. The hasSide property can define this entity as a member of a "Force" or "Side," i.e. a team of entities that are friendly to each other and are all friendly, hostile or neutral to other defined teams. ForceSides are abstract objects. The friend/hostile/... relationship of this entity with others can be specfied with hasAllegianceRelationship properties and/or the hasSide property. The hasSide property can define this entity as a member of a "Force" or "Side," i.e. a team of entities that are friendly to each other and are all friendly, hostile or neutral to other defined teams. ForceSides are abstract objects.
The entity can also have friendly, hostile or neutral relationships with specific other objects as defined by its hasAllegianceRelationship properties. These can be used in place of ForceSide membership, or they can be used along with ForceSide memberships to define exceptions. The entity can also have friendly, hostile or neutral relationships with specific other objects as defined by its hasAllegianceRelationship properties. These can be used in place of ForceSide membership, or they can be used along with ForceSide memberships to define exceptions.
   
The isAffiliatedWith property defines organizations that this entity is affiliated with. These organizations are abstract; they are not actors. The isAffiliatedWith property defines organizations that this entity is affiliated with. These organizations are abstract; they are not actors.
   
C2 structures can be defined by using the hasSuperior property.</xs:documentation>  C2 structures can be defined by using the hasSuperior property.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityDescriptor</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityDescriptor</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="AffiliatedWith" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="AffiliatedWith" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="AllegianceRelationship" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="AllegianceRelationship" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="CommunicationsNetwork" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="CommunicationsNetwork" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Side" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Side" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Superior" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Superior" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="EntityHealthStatusType">     <xs:complexType name="EntityHealthStatusType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A representation of the health status of an entity. Subclasses for representing health as Operational Status, a count of some salient resource, or a percentage of full strength.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A representation of the health status of an entity. Subclasses for representing health as Operational Status, a count of some salient resource, or a percentage of full strength.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityHealthStatus</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityHealthStatus</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="OperationalStatus"/>           <xs:element ref="OperationalStatus"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Resources"/>           <xs:element ref="Resources"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Strength"/>           <xs:element ref="Strength"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:group name="EntityStateGroup">     <xs:group name="EntityStateGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Dynamic state information for entities in the exercise.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Dynamic state information for entities in the exercise.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityState</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityState</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="DateTime" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="DateTime" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group> 
   <xs:complexType name="EntityStateType">     <xs:complexType name="EntityStateType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Dynamic state information for entities in the exercise.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Dynamic state information for entities in the exercise.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityState</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EntityState</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="PhysicalState"/>           <xs:element ref="PhysicalState"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="EnvironmentalObjectType">     <xs:complexType name="EnvironmentalObjectType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A specified region in which the environment (e.g. weather, ocean state) is defined by a particular Environmental State.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A specified region in which the environment (e.g. weather, ocean state) is defined by a particular Environmental State.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EnvironmentalObject</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EnvironmentalObject</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="EntityGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="EntityGroup"/> 
         <xs:group ref="PhysicalEntityGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="PhysicalEntityGroup"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="EnvironmentalObject" type="EnvironmentalObjectType"/>     <xs:element name="EnvironmentalObject" type="EnvironmentalObjectType"/> 
   <xs:simpleType name="EthnicGroupCodeType">     <xs:simpleType name="EthnicGroupCodeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Alphabetic code up to six letters, describing an ethnic group, obtained from JC3IEDM AffiliationEthnicGroupCode-3.0.2</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Alphabetic code up to six letters, describing an ethnic group, obtained from JC3IEDM AffiliationEthnicGroupCode-3.0.2</xs:documentation>

      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:complexType name="EulerAnglesType">     <xs:complexType name="EulerAnglesType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Representation of the orientation of an entity in 3D space expressed as Euler angles (DIS). This orientation will be used primarily for initialization of simulation entities, especially those that are not clamped to the ground.          <xs:documentation>Representation of the orientation of an entity in 3D space expressed as Euler angles (DIS). This orientation will be used primarily for initialization of simulation entities, especially those that are not clamped to the ground.
This orienation indicates rotations from a geocentric coordinate system.</xs:documentation>  This orienation indicates rotations from a geocentric coordinate system.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EulerAngles</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EulerAngles</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="OrientationGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="OrientationGroup"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Phi" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Phi" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Psi" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Psi" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Theta" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Theta" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>

   <xs:complexType name="EventType">     <xs:complexType name="EventType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An ACTION that is an incident, phenomenon, or occasion. May or may not be the result of an agent's action.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An ACTION that is an incident, phenomenon, or occasion. May or may not be the result of an agent's action.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Event</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Event</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="ActionGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="ActionGroup"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Duration" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Duration" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="EventCode" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="EventCode" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="StartTime" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="StartTime" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:simpleType name="EventCodeType">     <xs:simpleType name="EventCodeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The type of event.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The type of event.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EventCode</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#EventCode</xs:documentation>

   <xs:complexType name="GeodeticCoordinateType">     <xs:complexType name="GeodeticCoordinateType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A location defined by latitude, longitude, and altitude.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A location defined by latitude, longitude, and altitude.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#GeodeticCoordinate</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#GeodeticCoordinate</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="AltitudeAGL" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="AltitudeAGL" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="AltitudeMSL" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="AltitudeMSL" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Latitude" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Latitude" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Longitude" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Longitude" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="GeodeticCoordinate" type="GeodeticCoordinateType"/>     <xs:element name="GeodeticCoordinate" type="GeodeticCoordinateType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="GeographicFeatureType">     <xs:complexType name="GeographicFeatureType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A geographic feature generally used for location reference. For example a hill, river, etc.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A geographic feature generally used for location reference. For example a hill, river, etc.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#GeographicFeature</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#GeographicFeature</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="EntityGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="EntityGroup"/> 
         <xs:group ref="PhysicalEntityGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="PhysicalEntityGroup"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="GeographicFeature" type="GeographicFeatureType"/>     <xs:element name="GeographicFeature" type="GeographicFeatureType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="HeadingType">     <xs:complexType name="HeadingType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Heading is the rotation clockwise relative to local grid north. This is a rotation about one axis; other axis orientations are unspecified, and if a system needs values they can be assumed to be zero or appropriate angles to clamp the entity to the ground.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Heading is the rotation clockwise relative to local grid north. This is a rotation about one axis; other axis orientations are unspecified, and if a system needs values they can be assumed to be zero or appropriate angles to clamp the entity to the ground.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Heading</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Heading</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="OrientationGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="OrientationGroup"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="Heading" type="HeadingType"/>     <xs:element name="Heading" type="HeadingType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="InitializationCompleteType">     <xs:complexType name="InitializationCompleteType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This C2SIM message is sent by each participating system in a C2SIM coalition of systems in response to the C2SIM initialization message, after that system has completed its initialization processing.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This C2SIM message is sent by each participating system in a C2SIM coalition of systems in response to the C2SIM initialization message, after that system has completed its initialization processing.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#InitializationComplete</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#InitializationComplete</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="InitializationComplete" type="InitializationCompleteType"/>     <xs:element name="InitializationComplete" type="InitializationCompleteType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="InitializationConceptType">     <xs:complexType name="InitializationConceptType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This parent class collects together classes that are necessary for the initialization of a scenario.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This parent class collects together classes that are necessary for the initialization of a scenario.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#InitializationConcept</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#InitializationConcept</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="InitializationDataFile"/>           <xs:element ref="InitializationDataFile"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ObjectDefinitions"/>           <xs:element ref="ObjectDefinitions"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ScenarioSetting"/>           <xs:element ref="ScenarioSetting"/> 
         <xs:element ref="SystemEntityList"/>           <xs:element ref="SystemEntityList"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="InitializationConcept" type="InitializationConceptType"/>     <xs:element name="InitializationConcept" type="InitializationConceptType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="InitializationDataFileType">     <xs:complexType name="InitializationDataFileType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Defines a file of supplementary data that is used to initialize the scenario. This file is generally system-specific, for example a terrain database file or a mapping from C2SIM entity types to local entity type enumerations. It could apply to "all" systems.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Defines a file of supplementary data that is used to initialize the scenario. This file is generally system-specific, for example a terrain database file or a mapping from C2SIM entity types to local entity type enumerations. It could apply to "all" systems.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#InitializationDataFile</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#InitializationDataFile</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="IntializationFileType" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="IntializationFileType" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Name" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="SystemName" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="SystemName" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="IntervalTimeType">     <xs:complexType name="IntervalTimeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An interval defined by a start and end dateTime. Note that 2 of the 3 properties must be specified.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An interval defined by a start and end dateTime. Note that 2 of the 3 properties must be specified.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#IntervalTime</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#IntervalTime</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="Duration" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Duration" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="EndTime" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="EndTime" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="StartTime" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="StartTime" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="IntervalTime" type="IntervalTimeType"/>     <xs:element name="IntervalTime" type="IntervalTimeType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="LocationType">     <xs:complexType name="LocationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A representation of location in space. Can be a coordinate value, a reference to an entity location, or a location relative to another location.          <xs:documentation>A representation of location in space. Can be a coordinate value, a reference to an entity location, or a location relative to another location.
   
An optional altitude representation code indicates whether this is a fully specified point in 3 dimensions, or whether the altitude encoded in the class should be ignored or set to some other reference. If the code is not present, then the location is assumed to be a fully specified 3D point. An optional altitude representation code indicates whether this is a fully specified point in 3 dimensions, or whether the altitude encoded in the class should be ignored or set to some other reference. If the code is not present, then the location is assumed to be a fully specified 3D point.
   
The location of a CollectiveEntity may represent the center of mass or headquarters location in the case of an organization.</xs:documentation>  The location of a CollectiveEntity may represent the center of mass or headquarters location in the case of an organization.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Location</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#Location</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="GeodeticCoordinate"/>           <xs:element ref="GeodeticCoordinate"/> 
         <xs:element ref="RelativeLocation"/>           <xs:element ref="RelativeLocation"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="MagicMoveType">     <xs:complexType name="MagicMoveType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>System command used as an exercise control capabiliity to immediately reposition some entity (i.e., without going through a simulated movement). The hasEntityReference property provides the UUID of the entity being repositioned. The hasTasker property provides the UUID of the entity ordering the repositioning. The hasTaskID property gives the reference to a Task that the repositioning may be associated with.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>System command used as an exercise control capabiliity to immediately reposition some entity (i.e., without going through a simulated movement). The hasEntityReference property provides the UUID of the entity being repositioned. The hasTasker property provides the UUID of the entity ordering the repositioning. The hasTaskID property gives the reference to a Task that the repositioning may be associated with.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MagicMove</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MagicMove</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="EntityReference" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="EntityReference" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Location" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Location" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="TaskID" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="TaskID" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Tasker" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="Tasker" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="MagicMove" type="MagicMoveType"/>     <xs:element name="MagicMove" type="MagicMoveType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="MapGraphicType">     <xs:complexType name="MapGraphicType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Graphics on a map that define locations, lines, areas, actions, etc. The location of the graphic is the location in the real world represented on the map. The extent is the set of handle points or vertices that define its shape and size. This class includes task graphics, METOC graphics, and MOOTW graphics.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Graphics on a map that define locations, lines, areas, actions, etc. The location of the graphic is the location in the real world represented on the map. The extent is the set of handle points or vertices that define its shape and size. This class includes task graphics, METOC graphics, and MOOTW graphics.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MapGraphic</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MapGraphic</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="METOCGraphic"/>           <xs:element ref="METOCGraphic"/> 
         <xs:element ref="TacticalGraphic"/>           <xs:element ref="TacticalGraphic"/> 
         <xs:element ref="UnitSymbol"/>           <xs:element ref="UnitSymbol"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="MapGraphic" type="MapGraphicType"/>     <xs:element name="MapGraphic" type="MapGraphicType"/>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Any C2SIM message that is sent between host systems.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Any C2SIM message that is sent between host systems.</xs:documentation>

      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence>

         <xs:element ref="MessageBody" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="MessageBody" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The structure for sending domain messages: orders, reports, and acknowledgements.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The structure for sending domain messages: orders, reports, and acknowledgements.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MessageBody</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MessageBody</xs:documentation>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Enumerations used in messages.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Enumerations used in messages.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MessageCode</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MessageCode</xs:documentation>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This parent class collects together classes that define the basic structure of messages used in C2SIM.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This parent class collects together classes that define the basic structure of messages used in C2SIM.</xs:documentation>

   <xs:complexType name="MilitaryOrganizationType">     <xs:complexType name="MilitaryOrganizationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A collective entity that is a military organization (i.e., a unit).</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A collective entity that is a military organization (i.e., a unit).</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MilitaryOrganization</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#MilitaryOrganization</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="Unit"/>           <xs:element ref="Unit"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="MilitaryOrganization" type="MilitaryOrganizationType"/>     <xs:element name="MilitaryOrganization" type="MilitaryOrganizationType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="NamedEntityTypeType">     <xs:complexType name="NamedEntityTypeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The definition of an entity type with a unique identifier defined with a pair: namespace, name.          <xs:documentation>The definition of an entity type with a unique identifier defined with a pair: namespace, name.
The namespace and the name are free strings, and must be agreed in advance in the federation agreement template among the systems. For example the namespace could reference a standard, a format, or a simulation involved in the federation: openflight, app6d-sidc, sword, etc.</xs:documentation>  The namespace and the name are free strings, and must be agreed in advance in the federation agreement template among the systems. For example the namespace could reference a standard, a format, or a simulation involved in the federation: openflight, app6d-sidc, sword, etc.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#NamedEntityType</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#NamedEntityType</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="EntityTypeName" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="EntityTypeName" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="EntityTypeNamespace" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="EntityTypeNamespace" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="NonMilitaryOrganizationType">     <xs:complexType name="NonMilitaryOrganizationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An organization operating external to military authority (e.g., law enforcement, non-governmental organizations).</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An organization operating external to military authority (e.g., law enforcement, non-governmental organizations).</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#NonMilitaryOrganization</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#NonMilitaryOrganization</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="NonMilitaryOrganization" type="NonMilitaryOrganizationType"/>     <xs:element name="NonMilitaryOrganization" type="NonMilitaryOrganizationType"/>

         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#ObjectDefinitions</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#ObjectDefinitions</xs:documentation>

         <xs:documentation>This message is sent by each system to a marshalling server upon receipt of a SubmitInitialization message.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This message is sent by each system to a marshalling server upon receipt of a SubmitInitialization message.</xs:documentation>

   <xs:complexType name="OperationalStatusType">     <xs:complexType name="OperationalStatusType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An enumeration indicating degree of partial capability.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An enumeration indicating degree of partial capability.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OperationalStatus</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OperationalStatus</xs:documentation>

      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:simpleType name="OperationalStatusCodeType">     <xs:simpleType name="OperationalStatusCodeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An enumeration indicating the general level of capability of an entity relative to its greatest expected capability.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An enumeration indicating the general level of capability of an entity relative to its greatest expected capability.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OperationalStatusCode</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OperationalStatusCode</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="xs:string">        <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> 
         <xs:enumeration value="FullyOperational"/>           <xs:enumeration value="FullyOperational"/> 
         <xs:enumeration value="MostlyOperational"/>           <xs:enumeration value="MostlyOperational"/> 
         <xs:enumeration value="NotOperational"/>           <xs:enumeration value="NotOperational"/> 
         <xs:enumeration value="PartlyOperational"/>           <xs:enumeration value="PartlyOperational"/> 
      </xs:restriction>        </xs:restriction> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:complexType name="OrderBodyType">     <xs:complexType name="OrderBodyType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The structure for sending order messages.          <xs:documentation>The structure for sending order messages.
This message may define tasks, or it may refer to tasks defined elsewhere (such as in an initialization message). This message may define tasks, or it may refer to tasks defined elsewhere (such as in an initialization message).
An order generally comes from an entity with command authority and thus is expected to be obeyed if it is physically possible to do so.</xs:documentation>  An order generally comes from an entity with command authority and thus is expected to be obeyed if it is physically possible to do so.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OrderBody</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OrderBody</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="DomainMessageBodyGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="DomainMessageBodyGroup"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Entity" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="Entity" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="IssuedTime" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="IssuedTime" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="OrderID" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="OrderID" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="RequestingEntity" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="RequestingEntity" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="Task" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="Task" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="TaskReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="TaskReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="OrderBody" type="OrderBodyType"/>     <xs:element name="OrderBody" type="OrderBodyType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="OrganizationCodeType">     <xs:complexType name="OrganizationCodeType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>These provide enumerations of alphabetic text codes identifying characteristics of organizations.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>These provide enumerations of alphabetic text codes identifying characteristics of organizations.</xs:documentation>

      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice>

         <xs:documentation>Alphabetic code up to six letters describing the type of an organization, obtained from JC3IEDM-3.0.2 GroupOrganisationTypeCategoryCode</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Alphabetic code up to six letters describing the type of an organization, obtained from JC3IEDM-3.0.2 GroupOrganisationTypeCategoryCode</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OrganizationTypeCode</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#OrganizationTypeCode</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

         <xs:enumeration value="CIVCON"/>           <xs:enumeration value="CIVCON"/>
