   <xs:complexType name="TaskRequestContentType">     <xs:complexType name="TaskRequestContentType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A request for an Actor to perform a Task. The Actor to perform the Task is identified by the hasPerformingEntity property of the Task.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A request for an Actor to perform a Task. The Actor to perform the Task is identified by the hasPerformingEntity property of the Task.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#TaskRequestContent</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#TaskRequestContent</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="Task" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="Task" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>

   <xs:complexType name="TaskStatusType">     <xs:complexType name="TaskStatusType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>The status of a task, provided before, during or after a task is executed.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>The status of a task, provided before, during or after a task is executed.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#TaskStatus</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/C2SIM#TaskStatus</xs:documentation>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Time concepts include instants in time, durations of time, and intervals between two times.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Time concepts include instants in time, durations of time, and intervals between two times.</xs:documentation>

   <xs:group name="TimeInstantGroup">     <xs:group name="TimeInstantGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A specific time expressed in seconds, i.e. with no date. The generator and user of this time must understand a common reference point, for example the start of the scenario.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A specific time expressed in seconds, i.e. with no date. The generator and user of this time must understand a common reference point, for example the start of the scenario.</xs:documentation>

      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group>

   <xs:complexType name="EventTriggerType">     <xs:complexType name="EventTriggerType">

         <xs:documentation>A trigger to start a plan phase that is based on the time of an event.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A trigger to start a plan phase that is based on the time of an event.</xs:documentation>

   <xs:complexType name="ManeuverWarfareTaskType">     <xs:complexType name="ManeuverWarfareTaskType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A task with additional properties for a Maneuver Warfare Task, including a definition of rules of engagement, and a relationship to another task.          <xs:documentation>A task with additional properties for a Maneuver Warfare Task, including a definition of rules of engagement, and a relationship to another task.
   
Note that the enumerated individuals in TaskActionCode have been expanded to include tasks defined by  the Joint Consultation Command Control Information Exchange Data Model (JC3IEDM) developed by the Multilateral Interoperability Programme (MIP).</xs:documentation>  Note that the enumerated individuals in TaskActionCode have been expanded to include tasks defined by  the Joint Consultation Command Control Information Exchange Data Model (JC3IEDM) developed by the Multilateral Interoperability Programme (MIP).</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#ManeuverWarfareTask</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#ManeuverWarfareTask</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="ActionGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="ActionGroup"/> 
         <xs:group ref="TaskGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="TaskGroup"/>

      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A RuleofEngagement that is based on a standard code describing engagement conditions.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A RuleofEngagement that is based on a standard code describing engagement conditions.</xs:documentation>

   <xs:complexType name="OnOrderTriggerType">     <xs:complexType name="OnOrderTriggerType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A trigger for the execution of a plan phase that is based on the start time of a task. Typically this task will be defined in an order and will have a TaskActionCode of ExecutePlanPhase.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A trigger for the execution of a plan phase that is based on the start time of a task. Typically this task will be defined in an order and will have a TaskActionCode of ExecutePlanPhase.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#OnOrderTrigger</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#OnOrderTrigger</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="TaskReference" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="TaskReference" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="OnOrderTrigger" type="OnOrderTriggerType"/>     <xs:element name="OnOrderTrigger" type="OnOrderTriggerType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="PlanBodyType">     <xs:complexType name="PlanBodyType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This message orders the recipient unit to begin execution of the indicated plan.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This message orders the recipient unit to begin execution of the indicated plan.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanBody</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanBody</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="DomainMessageBodyGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="DomainMessageBodyGroup"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PlanPhase" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="PlanPhase" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ToBeExecutedNow" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="ToBeExecutedNow" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="PlanBody" type="PlanBodyType"/>     <xs:element name="PlanBody" type="PlanBodyType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="PlanPhaseType">     <xs:complexType name="PlanPhaseType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A plan for a unit. A PlanPhase may a single task for a unit, or tasks for multiple units (one each), or (recursively) multiple subphases.          <xs:documentation>A plan for a unit. A PlanPhase may a single task for a unit, or tasks for multiple units (one each), or (recursively) multiple subphases.
Phases have completion conditions that indicate when they are complete, as well as Boolean flags that indicate whether the phase is active and whether it is complete. Phases have completion conditions that indicate when they are complete, as well as Boolean flags that indicate whether the phase is active and whether it is complete.
Phases have trigger conditions that indicate when they start.  Phases may start on the completion of another phase--thus forming a task sequence--on receipt of an order, or at the time of an Event. Phases have trigger conditions that indicate when they start.  Phases may start on the completion of another phase--thus forming a task sequence--on receipt of an order, or at the time of an Event.
 A sequence of tasks for one unit is normally represented as a sequence of subphases in order to express the triggering conditions for each task; however a set of tasks for a unit may be included in a phase and executed with some implicit sequencing such as task time.</xs:documentation>   A sequence of tasks for one unit is normally represented as a sequence of subphases in order to express the triggering conditions for each task; however a set of tasks for a unit may be included in a phase and executed with some implicit sequencing such as task time.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanPhase</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanPhase</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseCompletionCondition" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseCompletionCondition" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseTrigger" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="PlanPhaseTrigger" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="SubPhase" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="SubPhase" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
         <xs:element ref="TaskReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="TaskReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:simpleType name="PlanPhaseCompletionConditionType">     <xs:simpleType name="PlanPhaseCompletionConditionType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A code defining the condition to be used to test the tasks of a plan phase to determine whether the phase is considered to be complete.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A code defining the condition to be used to test the tasks of a plan phase to determine whether the phase is considered to be complete.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanPhaseCompletionCondition</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanPhaseCompletionCondition</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:restriction base="xs:string">        <xs:restriction base="xs:string"> 
         <xs:enumeration value="AllTasksComplete"/>           <xs:enumeration value="AllTasksComplete"/> 
         <xs:enumeration value="OneTaskComplete"/>           <xs:enumeration value="OneTaskComplete"/> 
         <xs:enumeration value="OtherOrderReceived"/>           <xs:enumeration value="OtherOrderReceived"/> 
      </xs:restriction>        </xs:restriction> 
   </xs:simpleType>     </xs:simpleType> 
   <xs:complexType name="PlanPhaseTriggerType">     <xs:complexType name="PlanPhaseTriggerType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This is a condition that causes the start of execution of a PlanPhase.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This is a condition that causes the start of execution of a PlanPhase.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanPhaseTrigger</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PlanPhaseTrigger</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="EventTrigger"/>           <xs:element ref="EventTrigger"/> 
         <xs:element ref="OnOrderTrigger"/>           <xs:element ref="OnOrderTrigger"/> 
         <xs:element ref="PriorPhaseCompletionTrigger"/>           <xs:element ref="PriorPhaseCompletionTrigger"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="PriorPhaseCompletionTriggerType">     <xs:complexType name="PriorPhaseCompletionTriggerType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A plan phase trigger that is based on the completion of another plan phase (typically, the phase immediately before this one in a plan).</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A plan phase trigger that is based on the completion of another plan phase (typically, the phase immediately before this one in a plan).</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PriorPhaseCompletionTrigger</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#PriorPhaseCompletionTrigger</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="TriggerPhase" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="TriggerPhase" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="PriorPhaseCompletionTrigger"     <xs:element name="PriorPhaseCompletionTrigger" 
               type="PriorPhaseCompletionTriggerType"/>                 type="PriorPhaseCompletionTriggerType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="RuleOfEngagementType">     <xs:complexType name="RuleOfEngagementType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>A rule that describes the conditions under which weapons can be used to engage other forces. This class is a collection class; different types of ROEs will be subclasses of this class.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>A rule that describes the conditions under which weapons can be used to engage other forces. This class is a collection class; different types of ROEs will be subclasses of this class.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#RuleOfEngagement</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/lox#RuleOfEngagement</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>

   <xs:complexType name="ForceSideType">     <xs:complexType name="ForceSideType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>Class for a collection of Entities that have the same allegiance. I.e. they are all friendly to each other. For example, ForceSides could be defined for "BLUFOR", "OPFOR", and "CIVILIAN." Entities could be associated with one of these ForceSides through the hasSide property of their EntityDescriptor object property.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>Class for a collection of Entities that have the same allegiance. I.e. they are all friendly to each other. For example, ForceSides could be defined for "BLUFOR", "OPFOR", and "CIVILIAN." Entities could be associated with one of these ForceSides through the hasSide property of their EntityDescriptor object property.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#ForceSide</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#ForceSide</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:group ref="AbstractObjectGroup"/>           <xs:group ref="AbstractObjectGroup"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ForceSideRelation" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="ForceSideRelation" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:element name="ForceSide" type="ForceSideType"/>     <xs:element name="ForceSide" type="ForceSideType"/> 
   <xs:complexType name="ForceSideRelationType">     <xs:complexType name="ForceSideRelationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This relation describes the hostility relationship of a military force toward another force. The force that has this relationship as its property is the "military force", while the force named in the hasOtherSide property of this relationship is "another force."</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>This relation describes the hostility relationship of a military force toward another force. The force that has this relationship as its property is the "military force", while the force named in the hasOtherSide property of this relationship is "another force."</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#ForceSideRelation</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#ForceSideRelation</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation>

      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An observation of the health state of a subject; for example operational status or resource count.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An observation of the health state of a subject; for example operational status or resource count.</xs:documentation>

      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>An observation of the unit name, a vehicle marking, a force side, and/or a hostility status of the observed entity.</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>An observation of the unit name, a vehicle marking, a force side, and/or a hostility status of the observed entity.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#NameObservation</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#NameObservation</xs:documentation>

   <xs:element name="NameObservation" type="NameObservationType"/>     <xs:element name="NameObservation" type="NameObservationType"/> 
   <xs:group name="ObservationGroup">     <xs:group name="ObservationGroup"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This is an observation of the state of something in the world; generally an actor, but possibly anything that is an entity. Subclasses define specific observations that can be made.          <xs:documentation>This is an observation of the state of something in the world; generally an actor, but possibly anything that is an entity. Subclasses define specific observations that can be made.
The subclasses defined here are sufficient to create a report with Size, Activity, Location, Unit Identification, Time, and Equipment, or some subset of that.</xs:documentation>  The subclasses defined here are sufficient to create a report with Size, Activity, Location, Unit Identification, Time, and Equipment, or some subset of that.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#Observation</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#Observation</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence> 
         <xs:element ref="ActorReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="ActorReference" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="ConfidenceLevel" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="ConfidenceLevel" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
         <xs:element ref="UncertaintyInterval" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/>           <xs:element ref="UncertaintyInterval" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:group>     </xs:group> 
   <xs:complexType name="ObservationType">     <xs:complexType name="ObservationType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This is an observation of the state of something in the world; generally an actor, but possibly anything that is an entity. Subclasses define specific observations that can be made.          <xs:documentation>This is an observation of the state of something in the world; generally an actor, but possibly anything that is an entity. Subclasses define specific observations that can be made.
The subclasses defined here are sufficient to create a report with Size, Activity, Location, Unit Identification, Time, and Equipment, or some subset of that.</xs:documentation>  The subclasses defined here are sufficient to create a report with Size, Activity, Location, Unit Identification, Time, and Equipment, or some subset of that.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#Observation</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#Observation</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:choice>        <xs:choice> 
         <xs:element ref="ActivityObservation"/>           <xs:element ref="ActivityObservation"/> 
         <xs:element ref="HealthObservation"/>           <xs:element ref="HealthObservation"/> 
         <xs:element ref="LocationObservation"/>           <xs:element ref="LocationObservation"/>

         <xs:element ref="SubjectTypeObservation"/>           <xs:element ref="SubjectTypeObservation"/> 
      </xs:choice>        </xs:choice> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType> 
   <xs:complexType name="ObservationReportContentType">     <xs:complexType name="ObservationReportContentType"> 
      <xs:annotation>        <xs:annotation> 
         <xs:documentation>This report contains an observation of some aspect of a subject--not necessarily including the location, health, or other facts that are in a position report.          <xs:documentation>This report contains an observation of some aspect of a subject--not necessarily including the location, health, or other facts that are in a position report.
Multiple observations all refer to the same observed actor.</xs:documentation>  Multiple observations all refer to the same observed actor.</xs:documentation> 
         <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#ObservationReportContent</xs:documentation>           <xs:documentation>http://www.sisostds.org/ontologies/smx#ObservationReportContent</xs:documentation> 
      </xs:annotation>        </xs:annotation> 
      <xs:sequence>        <xs:sequence>

         <xs:element ref="Observation" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>           <xs:element ref="Observation" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> 
      </xs:sequence>        </xs:sequence> 
   </xs:complexType>     </xs:complexType>
